evaluations.name: Event-Driven Consumption Spike
evaluations.recommendation: Link building control system to event calendar for automated scale-up.

evaluations.name: Evening and Weekend Dominance
evaluations.recommendation: Optimize pre-event preparation time — start HVAC 90 min before, not 3h.

evaluations.name: Demand Spike Detection
evaluations.message: Peak demand is 2.0x the average — opportunity to flatten load curve.
evaluations.recommendation: Implement demand response: stagger HVAC start, shift non-critical loads.

evaluations.name: Slow Post-Event Ramp-Down
evaluations.recommendation: Automate post-event shutdown sequence for HVAC and non-essential lighting.

evaluations.name: Weather Sensitivity Check
evaluations.message: Energy use is 30% correlated with outdoor temperature.
evaluations.recommendation: Optimize HVAC based on temperature forecast to reduce reactive energy spikes.

evaluations.name: High Night-Time Base Load
evaluations.message: Building is consuming 0.788% of daytime energy overnight.
evaluations.recommendation: Investigate equipment left running overnight. Schedule HVAC setback.

evaluations.name: High Consumption During Low-Occupancy Hours
evaluations.recommendation: Configure occupancy sensors and timer-based shutdowns.

alerts.name: Weather Sensitivity Check
alerts.message: Energy use is 30% correlated with outdoor temperature.
alerts.recommendation: Optimize HVAC based on temperature forecast to reduce reactive energy spikes.

alerts.name: High Night-Time Base Load
alerts.message: Building is consuming 0.788% of daytime energy overnight.
alerts.recommendation: Investigate equipment left running overnight. Schedule HVAC setback.
